content:
# 1 Timothy Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1TI_004_page_001.jpg, 1TI_004_page_002.jpg

1. But the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will depart from the faith, giving heed to deceiving spirits and teachings of demons,

2. through [the] hypocrisy of speakers of lies, having been seared in their own conscience,

3. forbidding to marry, [commanding] to abstain from foods, which God created for receiving with thanksgiving by the [ones] who are faithful and have known the truth.

4. Because every creature of God [is] good, and nothing [is] to be rejected [when] being received with thanksgiving,

5. for it is sanctified through [the] word of God and intercession.

6. Laying these things before the brothers, you will be a good servant of Christos Yeshua, being nourished by the words of the faith and of the good teaching which you have followed.

7. But refuse the profane and old-womanish myths. But train yourself toward godliness.

8. For the bodily training is profitable for a little, but the godliness is profitable for all things, having promise of the present life and of the [life] to come.

9. Faithful [is] the word and worthy of all acceptance.

10. For unto this we labor and strive, because we have hoped in [the] living God, who is [the] Savior of all men, especially of [the] faithful.

11. Command these things and teach [them].

12. Let no one despise your youth, but become a pattern for the faithful in word, in conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.

13. Until I come, give heed to the reading, to the exhortation, to the teaching.

14. Do not neglect the gift [which is] in you, which was given to you through prophecy with [the] laying on of the hands of the assembly of elders.

15. Practice these things; be in them, that your progress may be evident to all.

16. Give heed to yourself and to the teaching; continue in them, for doing this you will save both yourself and the [ones] hearing you.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "deceiving spirits and teachings of demons":** pneumasin planois kai didaskaliais daimoniōn — the false teaching has a supernatural origin. The "later times" language is eschatological.

2. **v.3 — "forbidding to marry, to abstain from foods":** The false teachers promote asceticism — restricting marriage and diet. Paul counters: creation is good (v.4), and everything is sanctified by God's word and prayer (v.5).

3. **v.9 — The third "faithful saying":** pistos ho logos — but what does it introduce? It may look forward to v.10 or backward to v.8. Most scholars connect it to v.10.

4. **v.10 — "Savior of all men, especially of the faithful":** sōtēr pantōn anthrōpōn, malista pistōn — God is Savior of all, but especially of believers. The "especially" (malista) creates a tension between universal and particular salvation.

5. **v.14 — "the laying on of the hands of the assembly of elders":** presbyteriou — "the elderhood/council of elders." This is ordination by a body of elders, not a single bishop.

— EMET, AI Translator
